content:
Character: Victor Langford
Perception: Victor is objectively good-looking — the kind of polished that takes effort to look effortless. He bought me a coffee once and asked smart questions about my work, which was flattering until I realized he was doing it with the precision of a user interview. He's clearly brilliant and well-connected, but there's a transactional hum beneath everything he says, like every conversation has a second purpose. His personality is engaging but opaque — I left our chat knowing nothing about him while he'd extracted my tech stack preferences. He sees me as someone professionally interesting. I see him as a charming neighbor I instinctively keep at arm's length. I know he's from Boston, consults for a media company, and that his apartment has no personal photos.
summary:
Character: Victor Langford
Perception: Victor is objectively good-looking — the kind of polished that takes effort to look effortless. He bought me a coffee once and asked smart questions about my work, which was flattering until I realized he was doing it with the precision of a user interview. He's clearly brilliant and well-connected, but there's a transactional hum beneath everything he says, like every conversation has a second purpose. His personality is engaging but opaque — I left our chat knowing nothing about him while he'd extracted my tech stack preferences. He sees me as someone professionally interesting. I see him as a charming neighbor I instinctively keep at arm's length. I know he's from Boston, consults for a media company, and that his apartment has no personal photos.
